channel.title: CyberNetSec - Patch Management
channel.description: Latest patch management threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch for Actively Exploited Exchange Server RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-17747)
item.description:
Microsoft has released an emergency, out-of-band security update for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server 2016 and 2019. Tracked as CVE-2026-17747 with a CVSS score of 9.8, the flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to gain code execution by sending a sp...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical Pre-Auth RCE Flaw in libssh2 Library Poses Widespread Risk
item.description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2026-55200, has been disclosed in libssh2, a widely used open-source SSH client library. The flaw, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale, is a pre-authentication heap buffer overflow that can be triggered by a malicious SSH server. Because libssh2 is e...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited PTC and Cisco Flaws to KEV Catalog, Mandates Federal Patching
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, confirming they are under active exploitation. The flaws are CVE-2026-12569, an improper input validation bug in PTC's Windchill and FlexPLM produc...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: 19-Year-Old 'CIFSwitch' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-46243) Gives Instant Root
item.description:
A critical, 19-year-old vulnerability has been discovered in the Linux kernel. Dubbed 'CIFSwitch' and tracked as CVE-2026-46243, the flaw allows any local user to gain immediate root privileges with a single command. The local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability affects default configurations o...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Google Chrome 149 Update Patches 4 Critical and 14 High-Severity Flaws
item.description:
Google has released a security update for its Chrome browser, version 149, to address 18 security vulnerabilities. The patch bundle includes fixes for four flaws rated Critical and fourteen rated High severity. A majority of the fixed vulnerabilities are use-after-free bugs in core components like W...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability

Read Full Article →

item.title: FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities Chained for Root-Level Takeover, Active Exploits in Wild
item.description:
Check Point Research reports that threat actors are actively exploiting a chain of three vulnerabilities in Fortinet's FortiSandbox product (CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, CVE-2026-25089). An unauthenticated attacker can chain these flaws to achieve path traversal and execute commands with root pri...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Unpatched "CosmicSting" Flaw Leaves 75% of Magento & Adobe Commerce Sites Open to RCE
item.description:
A critical XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability, dubbed 'CosmicSting' and tracked as CVE-2024-34102, is affecting Adobe Commerce and Magento platforms. With a CVSS score of 9.8, the flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to read sensitive server files. When chained with a second vulnerability (CVE...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Patch Actively Exploited Linux Kernel Flaw (CVE-2024-1086)
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel, tracked as CVE-2024-1086, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 7.8, is a use-after-free weakness in ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Hackers Actively Exploit Gravity SMTP Flaw (CVE-2026-4020) to Steal API Keys from 100K WordPress Sites
item.description:
A medium-severity information disclosure vulnerability in the Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin, CVE-2026-4020, is being actively and widely exploited by threat actors. The flaw affects up to 100,000 websites and allows an unauthenticated attacker to access a comprehensive system report by making a sing...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Splunk Scrambles to Patch Critical 9.8 CVSS Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated RCE
item.description:
Splunk has issued an emergency patch for a critical vulnerability, CVE-2026-20253, affecting Splunk Enterprise. The flaw, with a CVSS score of 9.8, resides in an insecure PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint, allowing unauthenticated attackers to perform arbitrary file operations and achieve remote c...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →
